role_summary: Mississippi civil rights movement participant; one half of the Hamilton/Ulmer joint oral history (catalog entry 4). The audit-canon resolved her name from 'Joanne' (a prior Whisper rendering) to 'JoeAnn' per the AUDIT_VS_LOC_DISAGREEMENTS report.
ai_reading: JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 4 (catalog slug 'audrey-nell-hamilton-and-joeann-anderson-ulmer'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 4 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 4
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: St. Augustine Foot Soldiers Monument, St. Augustine, Florida
gallery.caption: The St. Augustine Foot Soldiers Monument in St. Augustine, Florida, honoring participants in the St. Augustine movement, including JoeAnn Anderson Ulmer, one of the St. Augustine Four jailed after the 1963 Woolworth's lunch counter sit-in.
